title: SPA blueprint architecture contracts

rules.statement: Core (`system/core/`) must not depend on module PHP namespaces except paths explicitly allowlisted for incremental closure; new `use Modules\` or `require` of module runtime code in core is forbidden without updating the allowlist and justification in the same PR.
rules.rationale: Prevents entangling platform kernel with product modules so tenant and bootstrap contracts stay reviewable.

rules.statement: Modules must not bypass approved core contracts for tenant/org/branch scope: no silent downgrade from fail-closed tenant paths to repair/global SQL; repair and global entrypoints must remain explicitly named (see ROOT-04 in ROOT-CAUSE-REGISTER-01.md).
rules.rationale: Contract bypass is the dominant class of tenant regression; proof lives in mandatory verifiers and release law, not in a single grep.

rules.statement: Edits to hotspot paths (repositories, core, read-only verifiers, mandatory tenant gate script) must cite task id, risk removed, proof command(s), and ROOT family or NONE in `@release-proof` metadata.
rules.rationale: Stops vague claims and missing linkage to ROOT-01..ROOT-05 classification.

rules.statement: No silent fallback instantiation: tenant HTTP and default service paths must not instantiate alternate wide-scope repositories or bypass gates via generic factories without an explicit, named contract (aligned with ROOT-04 / ROOT-05).
rules.rationale: Automated detection of all factory patterns is noisy; architecture doc states the bar; proofs enforce critical paths.

rules.statement: Tenant, organization, and branch scope rules from charter and inventory docs are mandatory for data-plane changes; evidence is expected via existing read-only verifiers and release law, not ad-hoc testing only.

open_gaps: LAYERS.MODULES_USE_APPROVED_CONTRACTS has no single low-noise static rule; rely on review + verifiers.
RUNTIME.NO_SILENT_FALLBACK_INSTANTIATION is not fully machine-verifiable without high false-positive rate.
